---
title: "admixture"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(root.dir = "/Volumes/data/lh/popgen/admixture")
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

# Read the Q matrix (ancestry proportions)
q_matrix <- read.table("joint-all-recode-structure.4.Q", header = FALSE)

# Read sample names from the file
sample_names <- readLines("sample_identifiers_new.txt")  # Ensure this file contains one sample name per line

# Reshape the Q matrix to long format (each row will be a sample-population combination)
q_matrix_long <- melt(q_matrix, variable.name = "Population", value.name = "Proportion")

# Ensure the number of sample names matches the number of samples in the Q matrix
if (length(sample_names) != nrow(q_matrix)) {
  stop("The number of sample names does not match the number of samples in the Q matrix.")
}

# Assign the sample names
q_matrix_long$Sample <- rep(sample_names, times = ncol(q_matrix))  # Repeat for each population

# Create the stacked bar chart
plot <- ggplot(q_matrix_long, aes(x = factor(Sample), y = Proportion, fill = factor(Population))) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 6)) +  # Reduce font size for readability
  labs(title = "Ancestry Proportions", x = "Sample", y = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Population") +
  theme_minimal()

# Set the plot size for the print output (making it wider)
options(repr.plot.width = 30, repr.plot.height = 6)

# Print the plot
print(plot)
```


#sort

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)

# Read the Q matrix (ancestry proportions)
q_matrix <- read.table("joint-all-recode-structure.4.Q", header = FALSE)

# Read sample names from the correct file
sample_names <- readLines("sample_identifiers_new.txt")  # Corrected file name

# Reshape the Q matrix to long format (each row will be a sample-population combination)
q_matrix_long <- melt(q_matrix, variable.name = "Population", value.name = "Proportion")

# Ensure the number of sample names matches the number of samples in the Q matrix
if (length(sample_names) != nrow(q_matrix)) {
  stop("The number of sample names does not match the number of samples in the Q matrix.")
}

# Assign the sample names
q_matrix_long$Sample <- rep(sample_names, times = ncol(q_matrix))  # Repeat for each population

# Calculate the percentage of V1, V2, and V3 ancestry for each sample
v1_proportion <- q_matrix_long %>%
  filter(Population == "V1") %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(V1_Percentage = sum(Proportion))

v2_proportion <- q_matrix_long %>%
  filter(Population == "V2") %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(V2_Percentage = sum(Proportion))

v3_proportion <- q_matrix_long %>%
  filter(Population == "V3") %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(V3_Percentage = sum(Proportion))

# Merge the V1, V2, and V3 percentages with the main data frame
q_matrix_long <- merge(q_matrix_long, v1_proportion, by = "Sample")
q_matrix_long <- merge(q_matrix_long, v2_proportion, by = "Sample")
q_matrix_long <- merge(q_matrix_long, v3_proportion, by = "Sample")

# Create a sorting order based on V1 first and V3 second for 0% V1, then by V2 for 0% V1 and 0% V3
sorted_samples <- q_matrix_long %>%
  distinct(Sample, V1_Percentage, V2_Percentage, V3_Percentage) %>%
  arrange(desc(V1_Percentage), V3_Percentage, desc(V2_Percentage))  # Sort by V1 descending, then V3, then V2 for 0% V1 and V3

# Reorder the Sample factor according to the sorted order
q_matrix_long$Sample <- factor(q_matrix_long$Sample, levels = sorted_samples$Sample)

# Create the stacked bar chart
plot <- ggplot(q_matrix_long, aes(x = factor(Sample), y = Proportion, fill = factor(Population))) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6)) +  # Rotate the labels to 45 degrees
  labs(title = "Ancestry Proportions", x = "Sample", y = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Population") +
  theme_minimal()

# Set the plot size for the print output (making it wider)
options(repr.plot.width = 30, repr.plot.height = 6)

# Print the plot
print(plot)

```
```{r}
# Create the horizontal bar chart without the labels
plot <- ggplot(q_matrix_long, aes(y = factor(Sample), x = Proportion, fill = factor(Population))) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1, size = 6)) +  # Rotate the labels to be horizontal
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Population") +  # Keep the population legend
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Remove axis labels and title
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_blank(),
    # Optionally remove axis ticks
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    # Remove grid lines
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot)
```


```{r}
# Save the plot as a PNG file
ggsave("admixture-turned-new.png", plot = plot, width = 6.27, height = 9.7, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
plot <- ggplot(q_matrix_long, aes(y = factor(Sample), x = Proportion, fill = factor(Population))) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1, size = 6)) +  # Rotate the labels to be horizontal
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Population", labels = c("'indica' MJ", "'sativa' MJ", "HEMP", "HC_HEMP")) +  # Set custom labels
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Remove axis labels and title
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_blank(),
    # Optionally remove axis ticks
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    # Remove grid lines
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    # Set background to white
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white")
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot)

```
```{r}
# Save the plot as a PNG file
ggsave("admixture-turned-new.png", plot = plot, width = 6.27, height = 9.7, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```
